Task 20: Final Validation and Release Gate

Task 20: Final Validation and Release Gate
Task 20: Final Validation and Release Gate
REVIEW GATE 1 (after Task 4)
REVIEW GATE 1 (after Task 4)

tasks.goal: Preflight intake captures scope, autonomy level, budget, environment, access needs, MCP permissions, and approval boundaries when required.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 1 (after Task 4)
REVIEW GATE 1 (after Task 4)

tasks.goal: Dispatcher verifies required agents, skills, MCPs, reviewer independence, and fallback availability.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 1 (after Task 4)
REVIEW GATE 1 (after Task 4)

tasks.goal: No runtime can execute tasks yet without evaluator and policy guard.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 1 (after Task 4)

tasks.goal: User approved moving from specification to execution runtime.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 1 (after Task 4)

tasks.goal: User approval leases have explicit scope, expiry, and renewal behavior.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 1 (after Task 4)
REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: Independent evaluator is required for risky, user-facing, previously failed, shared-architecture, deployment, Docker, MCP, or production-intent tasks.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: Confidence calibration records false positives, reopened tasks, user corrections, and golden trace results.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)
REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)
REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: Stop command propagates cancellation to agents, MCP calls, browser sessions, and loop-owned processes without killing user-owned processes.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)
REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: Resume does not bypass policy-stopped runs.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: Resume does not reuse expired approval leases or approvals whose risk scope changed.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: Resume validates and migrates compatible state versions before dispatch.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: Resume reconciles side-effect ledger entries before retrying non-idempotent actions.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: Status files show loop, task, agent, score, budget, and stop reason.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: Open plan amendments cap run-level score below completion.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: Applicable rules and design-system constraints are loaded into each task.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: Rule precedence, design-system source of truth, and rule drift are captured before completion.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: Scenario matrix covers common request families before public release.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: Non-trivial tasks require context packs with memory, Code RAG, CodeGraph when applicable, and token estimates.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: Stale context or missing retrieval evidence caps confidence below completion.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: Durable memory writes are concise, evidence-linked, non-secret, and safe to reuse.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: Runtime environment classifier separates local, Docker, CI, staging, remote, and production actions.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: Platform command adapter handles Windows, POSIX, path quoting, env syntax, package manager detection, and process cleanup.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: MCP discovery and fallback behavior is captured before external validation claims.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: SDLC gates cap production-ready claims below completion until required evidence exists.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: Parallel write tasks use disjoint ownership, worktree isolation, or serialization.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: Audit trail and artifact provenance exist before completion can be claimed.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: Artifact retention, redaction, pruning, and privacy mode are defined before durable reports are written.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 2 (after Task 8)

tasks.goal: The command never documents provider bypass behavior.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: The default mode is bounded, visible, and stoppable.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: Cancellation tests prove stop behavior leaves a consistent partial report, side-effect ledger, and cleanup state.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: Required preflight cannot be bypassed for server, Docker, deploy, production-intent, external integration, or credential-dependent work.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: The command asks one question at a time and records preflight answers before dispatch.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: Missing access data blocks the run or creates a user-facing setup step instead of guessing credentials.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: Approval leases expire, are scoped to action and environment, and are renewed when risk changes.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: The loop can dispatch design, development, review, QA, security, and integration repair chains.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: Missing specialist agents, skills, or MCPs stop with `agent_unavailable` or create a `capability_gap` amendment.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: Medium/high-risk or previously failed work cannot be completed solely by the implementing agent's own score.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: A task is complete only at score >= 9.0 with required evidence.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: Repeated post-run failures after score >= 9.0 tighten the rubric or create calibration amendments before release.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: A run is complete only when all required tasks are complete and run-level score >= 9.0.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: A run cannot complete while discovered plan gaps, missing tasks, or unresolved amendments remain open.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: Old loop state cannot resume after plugin, schema, command, or rubric changes unless migration succeeds.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: A rule or design-system violation creates a remediation task or caps confidence below completion.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: Rule precedence conflicts and stale rule/design-system context create amendments or cap confidence below completion.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: Scenario coverage tests include plan execution, validation, repair, design, refactor, documentation, monorepo, flaky tests, missing credentials, and policy stop cases.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: Context economy tests prove the loop uses memory, Code RAG, CodeGraph, cached summaries, and targeted file reads before broad context expansion.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: Memory hygiene tests prove secrets, private data, large copied code, stale facts, and speculation are not persisted as durable memory.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: Token budget overrun stops the run, asks the user, or creates a context-reduction amendment.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: Server, Docker, CI, staging, remote server, and production deploy paths have separate risk classes, evidence requirements, and approval boundaries.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: Cross-platform tests prove generated verification commands work through the active shell adapter instead of assuming bash or PowerShell.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: External side effects have idempotency keys, verification, cleanup, and rollback or stop behavior.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: MCP validation paths use discovered user tools when available and record fallbacks when unavailable.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: SDLC gate tests prove production-ready work includes requirements, tests, supply-chain evidence, CI or local equivalent, security or privacy review, deploy or packaging evidence, rollback, smoke checks, and observability plan when relevant.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: Workspace isolation tests prove overlapping write sets cannot run in parallel without serialization or isolated workspaces.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: Audit trail tests prove every final claim links to task, agent, context, handoff, score, verification, approval, and rollback evidence.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: Artifact retention tests prove logs, context packs, screenshots, MCP outputs, and reports are redacted, pruned, and privacy-safe.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: E2E fixture tests prove local, Docker, MCP, missing credentials, flaky tests, and production approval scenarios behave correctly.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)

tasks.goal: High-risk or policy-sensitive tasks stop or ask for explicit user authorization.

source.section: REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)
REVIEW GATE 3 (before release)
